title: Mother Bar Auction Zones
focus_questions: Is the Mother Bar sitting inside a trading range, early trend continuation, or third-leg exhaustion?
Did price test an outer zone or trap zone, or is the operator trying to trade the middle of the micro-auction?

summary.thesis: A Mother Bar is a micro-auction map, not a universal trigger. Its outer zones, trap zones, and breakout odds only make sense after environment is classified.
summary.operator_translation: Do not trade the Mother Bar blindly. First decide whether the environment wants range fade, trend continuation, or late-leg reversal.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card when price is compressing inside a Mother Bar and the AI needs to explain which side of the range is actionable and which side should be suppressed.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: a Mother Bar defines a short-term micro-auction inside range, early trend pullback, or late wedge context. Required evidence: clear Mother Bar, context classification, and test of an outer zone or accepted breakout. Do not trade: from the middle of the range or by fading an already accepted breakout. Real trigger: outer-zone rejection in range context, trend-side hold in leg-one/leg-two context, or accepted breakout after competition-point confirmation. Failure point: context was misread or outside trade is clearly accepted against the chosen script.

when_relevant: A Mother Bar plus inside balance is defining a temporary micro-auction.
The operator needs to know whether to fade the edge, continue with trend, or prepare for late-leg reversal.
Price is approaching outer zones or competition points rather than remaining in the middle.
required_evidence: a clear Mother Bar defines the range being used
the environment can be classified as trading range, early trend, or late third-leg exhaustion
price tests an outer zone, trap zone, or accepted breakout condition instead of only hovering in the middle
confirmation_signals: outer-zone tests reject cleanly when the broader context is range-like
trend-side zone holds during leg-one or leg-two continuation context
body closes beyond the key competition point and later outside-hold quality supports breakout continuation
invalidation_signals: the market keeps trading in the middle of the mother range with no edge
range-fade logic is being used while outside trade is already accepted
the environment classification was wrong and the chosen side loses contextual support
no_trade_conditions: Do not open from the middle of the Mother Bar range.
Do not apply range-fade logic if the market is already accepting outside the Mother Bar.
Do not ignore whether the market is in early trend, late wedge, or plain chop.

entry_archetypes.name: outer-zone range fade
entry_archetypes.description: Enter from the outer trap zone when range context supports rejection back into the auction.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if body closes and outside-hold quality start proving breakout acceptance.

entry_archetypes.name: trend-side mother bar hold
entry_archetypes.description: Enter from the trend-side zone when a leg-one or leg-two pullback uses the Mother Bar as continuation structure.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the pullback is already deep enough to question continuation.

entry_archetypes.name: accepted mother bar breakout
entry_archetypes.description: Enter after competition-point confirmation and accepted outside trade beyond the Mother Bar.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if breakout is only a poke and not an accepted outside branch.

management_notes: The Mother Bar is a map, not a trade by itself.
The middle of the Mother Bar is usually poor location until breakout acceptance becomes clear.
As context shifts from range to trend or from trend to exhaustion, the valid side of the Mother Bar shifts too.
review_questions: Was the Mother Bar interpreted in the correct environment?
Did the operator trade an outer zone with edge, or the middle of the micro-auction?
Was breakout assumed before body closes and outside-hold quality proved it?
